basic_details.job_title: MP High Court Senior Computer Programmer Assistant Recruitment 2026: 130 Posts
basic_details.organization_name: High Court of Madhya Pradesh, Jabalpur
basic_details.short_information: The High Court of Madhya Pradesh, Jabalpur has released Advertisement No. 61/SCPA/Exam/2026 dated 17 July 2026, inviting applications for Direct Recruitment through Suitability Test for 130 posts of Senior Computer Programmer Assistant in District Courts of the State of Madhya Pradesh. This recruitment is conducted as a one-time measure under Rule 1(3) of the Madhya Pradesh District Court Technical Manpower (Appointment & Conditions of Service) Rules, 2019, and is open only to employees/officials of the High Court of Madhya Pradesh and District Courts (regular or outsourced) currently working

important_dates.date of preliminary screening examinatio: Not specified in the notification (shall be notified later)

application_fee.details: No fee is charged from candidates for appearing in this examination.

age_limit.details: As per the Madhya Pradesh Gazette notification dated 20.02.2026 (Bhopal order dated 17.02.2026, order number E-430953-2026-21-B(one)), the maximum age limit for candidates is 5 years more than the limit prescribed by the Madhya Pradesh General Administration Department Circular dated 4th July 2019. | The age limit is reckoned from 1st January of the year in which the recruitment process has been initiated, i.e., as on 01.01.2026. The notification does not specify a minimum age.

qualification.details: As per Schedule-I of the "Rules 2019," candidates must fulfil the following criteria for the post of Senior Computer Programmer Assistant: | B.E./B.Tech/B.Sc in Computer Science/IT/Electronics and Telecommunications, or B.C.A./M.C.A., or M.Sc. (Electronics/CS/IT), with at least 1 year of experience working on Linux/Open Source Software/Windows/DBMS/Software Development. Work experience of not less than 2 years in a District Court Establishment or the High Court of Madhya Pradesh. The candidate must not have been punished in any departmental enquiry, or ever punished for misconduct or any other reason. | Only persons having work experience of not less than 2 years in a District Court Establishment or High Court in the State of Madhya Pradesh are eligible to appear in the suitability test, subject to meeting the above essential qualifications. As per the Gazette notification dated 20.02.2026, all employees/officials of the High Court of Madhya Pradesh and District Courts in the state, irrespective of their designation or name of post, working in the IT domain - either as a regular employee or through outsourcing - can participate, as a one-time measure, in this recruitment process, subject to fulfilling the required qualifications and other conditions under the Rules 2019.

vacancy_details.Disability Category: Locomotor Disability (including Cerebral Palsy, Leprosy Cured, Dwarfism, Acid Attack Victims, Muscular Dystrophy)
Autism, Intellectual Disability, Specific Learning Disability and Mental Illness

pwd_wise_breakdown.disability_category: Locomotor Disability (including Cerebral Palsy, Leprosy Cured, Dwarfism, Acid Attack Victims, Muscular Dystrophy)
Autism, Intellectual Disability, Specific Learning Disability and Mental Illness

selection_process: Post Syllabus Marks Language

Senior Computer Programmer Assistant Fundamentals of IT & Operating Systems; Computer System Organization and Architecture; Computer Assembling & Troubleshooting; Computer Networks & Information Security; Programming Methodologies and Data Structures; Object Oriented Programming and Technology; Database Management System; Data Communication and Operation Systems; Internet of Things (IoT) & Applications; Cyber Security Framework; Web Design, Web Services and Web Applications Development; Software Engineering; System Administration 100 English
Grade (Yearly ACR) Marks
Outstanding (+A) 5
Very Good (A) 4
Good (B) 3
Fair (C) 2
Poor (D) 1
Grade (Monthly ACR) Marks
Average/Satisfactory/Above 0.4
Below Average/Not Satisfactory 0
The selection process consists of the following stages:
Preliminary Screening Examination (MCQ) Practical Examination Interview Past Performance Appraisal
The final merit list for selection is prepared on the basis of aggregate marks obtained in the Practical Examination
Interview
and Past Performance Appraisal
subject to securing the prescribed minimum marks. Marks obtained in the Preliminary Screening Examination are used only to shortlist candidates and are not counted in the final merit.
The question paper contains 100 multiple choice objective-type questions of 1 mark each
to be solved within 2 hours (120 minutes) on an OMR sheet
in English.
Candidates who secure a place in the merit list in each category
in the ratio of 1:3 against each post
shall be declared successful for the Practical Examination and Interview
including candidates securing equal marks to the last successful candidate in each category.
The Practical Examination may be held at the premises of the High Court of Madhya Pradesh
Jabalpur. Syllabus is the same as prescribed for the Preliminary Screening Examination. Maximum marks: 25. Date and time will be notified later on the official website.
The Interview carries a maximum of 15 marks and may be conducted at the premises of the High Court of Madhya Pradesh
Jabalpur. Candidates must carry their required original documents at the time of interview for verification.
Past Performance Appraisal carries a maximum of 10 marks
based on the candidate's last 2 years' appraisal reports (ACRs). Candidates who have discontinued their job but have the required work experience are also eligible.
how_to_apply: Applications must be submitted offline. The application form is not filled online.
Download the Application Form from the official website of the High Court of Madhya Pradesh (www.mphc.gov.in), along with the advertisement.
Take a printout and fill it in Block Letters, in English only. All fields are mandatory; write N/A where not applicable.
Sign the form and attach self-attested photocopies of all required documents/testimonials along with recent coloured photographs, serial-wise as per the checklist in the form.

Place the completed application and documents in an envelope marked "Direct Recruitment Through Suitability Test For The Post of Senior Computer Programmer Assistant in District Courts of the State of Madhya Pradesh - 2026" on top.
Send the envelope to: Registrar (Exam), Examination Section, Administrative Block, High Court of Madhya Pradesh, Jabalpur (M.P.)
Ensure the application reaches the Receipt Section on or before 10.08.2026. It is the candidate's sole responsibility to ensure timely receipt.

There is no provision for correction after submission of the application form. Applications received after the last date will not be entertained for any reason, including postal delay. Candidates are advised to keep a copy of their finally submitted application form.
important_instructions: Application forms found incomplete or not accompanied by the requisite documents will be summarily rejected without assigning any reason. After scrutiny, a list of eligible and ineligible candidates will be published on the High Court website. Permission to appear in the exam is provisional. If a candidate is found not to fulfil eligibility conditions, or certificates are found fake or fabricated, at any stage, the candidature will be rejected. No travelling expenses or allowances are payable for appearing in the Preliminary Screening Examination, Practical Examination, or Interview. There is no provision for rechecking of marks or revaluation of answer sheets at any stage, and re-examination is not permissible. Mobile phones, pagers, and other electronic/communication devices or calculators are prohibited inside the examination premises. Using unfair means, impersonation, submitting forged documents, or concealing material information can lead to prosecution and/or debarment, permanently or for a specified period, in addition to cancellation of candidature. Punishment under the Madhya Pradesh Recognized Examination Act, 1937 extends up to 3 years of imprisonment. Any corrigendum or clarification will be posted only on the official website of the High Court of Madhya Pradesh; this will be deemed sufficient notice to all candidates. Marks for all stages will be shown in a Score-card available on the official website; candidates will need to enter their credentials to view it. Records of the examination, including application forms, may be eliminated 6 months after declaration of the final result, unless a court case is pending.

content_sections.heading: MP High Court Senior Computer Programmer Assistant Recruitment 2026 - Overview

rows.details: High Court of Madhya Pradesh, Jabalpur

content_sections.heading: MP High Court Senior Computer Programmer Assistant Recruitment 2026 - Important Dates

rows.event: Commencement of Receipt of Application Forms
Last Date for Receiving Application Forms

rows.date: Not specified in the notification (shall be notified later)

content_sections.heading: Vacancy Details
content_sections.text: Out of the total 130 posts, 8 posts are reserved for Persons with Disabilities (PwBD), as follows:

rows.disability_category: Locomotor Disability (including Cerebral Palsy, Leprosy Cured, Dwarfism, Acid Attack Victims, Muscular Dystrophy)
Autism, Intellectual Disability, Specific Learning Disability and Mental Illness

content_sections.heading: Age Limit
content_sections.text: As per the Madhya Pradesh Gazette notification dated 20.02.2026 (Bhopal order dated 17.02.2026, order number E-430953-2026-21-B(one)), the maximum age limit for candidates is 5 years more than the limit prescribed by the Madhya Pradesh General Administration Department Circular dated 4th July 2019. The age limit is reckoned from 1st January of the year in which the recruitment process has been initiated, i.e., as on 01.01.2026. The notification does not specify a minimum age.

content_sections.heading: Eligibility and Educational Qualification
content_sections.text: As per Schedule-I of the "Rules 2019," candidates must fulfil the following criteria for the post of Senior Computer Programmer Assistant: B.E./B.Tech /B.Sc in Computer Science/IT/Electronics and Telecommunications, or B.C.A./M.C.A., or M.Sc. (Electronics/CS/IT), with at least 1 year of experience working on Linux/Open Source Software/Windows/DBMS/Software Development. Work experience of not less than 2 years in a District Court Establishment or the High Court of Madhya Pradesh. The candidate must not have been punished in any departmental enquiry, or ever punished for misconduct or any other reason. Only persons having work experience of not less than 2 years in a District Court Establishment or High Court in the State of Madhya Pradesh are eligible to appear in the suitability test, subject to meeting the above essential qualifications. As per the Gazette notification dated 20.02.2026, all employees/officials of the High Court of Madhya Pradesh and District Courts in the state, irrespective of their designation or name of post, working in the IT domain - either as a regular employee or through outsourcing - can participate, as a one-time measure, in this recruitment process, subject to fulfilling the required qualifications and other conditions under the Rules 2019.

content_sections.heading: No Objection Certificate (NOC)
content_sections.text: Candidates who are not currently employed in any District Court Establishment of Madhya Pradesh or the High Court of Madhya Pradesh must submit a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from the competent authority/employer at the time of filing the application form. Without this, the candidate will not be allowed to participate in the examination.

content_sections.heading: Selection Process
content_sections.text: The selection process consists of the following stages: Preliminary Screening Examination (MCQ) Practical Examination Interview Past Performance Appraisal The final merit list for selection is prepared on the basis of aggregate marks obtained in the Practical Examination, Interview, and Past Performance Appraisal, subject to securing the prescribed minimum marks. Marks obtained in the Preliminary Screening Examination are used only to shortlist candidates and are not counted in the final merit. The question paper contains 100 multiple choice objective-type questions of 1 mark each, to be solved within 2 hours (120 minutes) on an OMR sheet, in English. Candidates who secure a place in the merit list in each category, in the ratio of 1:3 against each post, shall be declared successful for the Practical Examination and Interview, including candidates securing equal marks to the last successful candidate in each category. The Practical Examination may be held at the premises of the High Court of Madhya Pradesh, Jabalpur. Syllabus is the same as prescribed for the Preliminary Screening Examination. Maximum marks: 25. Date and time will be notified later on the official website. The Interview carries a maximum of 15 marks and may be conducted at the premises of the High Court of Madhya Pradesh, Jabalpur. Candidates must carry their required original documents at the time of interview for verification. Past Performance Appraisal carries a maximum of 10 marks, based on the candidate's last 2 years' appraisal reports (ACRs). Candidates who have discontinued their job but have the required work experience are also eligible.

rows.syllabus: Fundamentals of IT & Operating Systems; Computer System Organization and Architecture; Computer Assembling & Troubleshooting; Computer Networks & Information Security; Programming Methodologies and Data Structures; Object Oriented Programming and Technology; Database Management System; Data Communication and Operation Systems; Internet of Things (IoT) & Applications; Cyber Security Framework; Web Design, Web Services and Web Applications Development; Software Engineering; System Administration

content_sections.heading: Application Fee
content_sections.text: No fee is charged from candidates for appearing in this examination.

content_sections.heading: Documents Required (Self-Attested Photocopies)
content_sections.text: Two recent passport-size coloured photographs of the candidate Documents related to Date of Birth Domicile Certificate of Madhya Pradesh (only for MP domicile candidates, issued by competent authority) Caste Certificate for OBC/SC/ST (issued by competent authority) Persons with Disability Certificate (issued by Medical Board), where applicable No Objection Certificate (NOC) of competent officer, where applicable Mark sheets, certificates and degrees of educational qualification (copies of every year/semester, if applicable) Other supporting documents, if any

content_sections.heading: How to Apply
content_sections.text: Applications must be submitted offline. The application form is not filled online. There is no provision for correction after submission of the application form. Applications received after the last date will not be entertained for any reason, including postal delay. Candidates are advised to keep a copy of their finally submitted application form.

content_sections.heading: Admit Card
content_sections.text: Admit Cards/Hall Tickets for the Preliminary Screening Examination will be generated by the High Court of Madhya Pradesh on its website (www.mphc.gov.in), at least 7 days prior to the date of examination, for candidates who have submitted complete applications within the prescribed period.

content_sections.heading: Other Important Instructions
content_sections.text: Application forms found incomplete or not accompanied by the requisite documents will be summarily rejected without assigning any reason. After scrutiny, a list of eligible and ineligible candidates will be published on the High Court website. Permission to appear in the exam is provisional. If a candidate is found not to fulfil eligibility conditions, or certificates are found fake or fabricated, at any stage, the candidature will be rejected. No travelling expenses or allowances are payable for appearing in the Preliminary Screening Examination, Practical Examination, or Interview. There is no provision for rechecking of marks or revaluation of answer sheets at any stage, and re-examination is not permissible. Mobile phones, pagers, and other electronic/communication devices or calculators are prohibited inside the examination premises. Using unfair means, impersonation, submitting forged documents, or concealing material information can lead to prosecution and/or debarment, permanently or for a specified period, in addition to cancellation of candidature. Punishment under the Madhya Pradesh Recognized Examination Act, 1937 extends up to 3 years of imprisonment. Any corrigendum or clarification will be posted only on the official website of the High Court of Madhya Pradesh; this will be deemed sufficient notice to all candidates. Marks for all stages will be shown in a Score-card available on the official website; candidates will need to enter their credentials to view it. Records of the examination, including application forms, may be eliminated 6 months after declaration of the final result, unless a court case is pending.

faq.question: Q1. Who is eligible to apply for the MP High Court Senior Computer Programmer Assistant recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Only employees/officials of the High Court of Madhya Pradesh and District Courts (regular or outsourced) working in the IT domain with at least 2 years of experience in a District Court Establishment or the High Court of Madhya Pradesh and the prescribed educational qualifications.

faq.question: Q2. What are the essential educational qualifications required for this post?
faq.answer: B.E./B.Tech/B.Sc (Computer Science/IT/Electronics & Telecommunications), or B.C.A./M.C.A., or M.Sc. (Electronics/CS/IT) plus at least 1 year of experience in Linux/Open Source/Windows/DBMS/Software Development.

faq.question: Q3. How many vacancies are available and what is the reservation breakdown?
faq.answer: Total vacancies: 130. Reserved: Unreserved 85, OBC 3, SC 21, ST 21. Eight posts are reserved for PwBD across specified categories.

faq.question: Q4. What is the application process and last date to apply?
faq.answer: Apply offline only. Download the form from www.mphc.gov.in, fill in block letters, attach self-attested documents, and send by post/hand-delivery to the Registrar (Exam), High Court of Madhya Pradesh, Jabalpur. Applications must reach by 10 August 2026.

faq.question: Q5. Is there an application fee for this recruitment?
faq.answer: No. There is no fee charged for appearing in this examination.

faq.question: Q6. What stages are included in the selection process?
faq.answer: Preliminary Screening Examination (MCQ) for shortlisting, Practical Examination, Interview, and Past Performance Appraisal. Final merit is based on Practical, Interview, and Past Performance (Prelim marks are only for shortlisting).

faq.question: Q7. What is the syllabus and format of the Preliminary Screening Examination?
faq.answer: 100 objective MCQs, 1 mark each, 2 hours, English, covering fundamentals of IT, OS, networks, programming, DBMS, cyber security, web development, IoT, software engineering, and system administration.

faq.question: Q8. What is the age limit for applicants?
faq.answer: As on 01.01.2026: UR - maximum 45 years; OBC/SC/ST/PH/Women - maximum 50 years (per Gazette orders). No minimum age specified in notification.

faq.question: Q9. What documents must be attached with the application form?
faq.answer: Two recent passport photos, DOB proof, MP domicile (if applicable), caste certificate (if applicable), PwBD certificate (if applicable), NOC (if currently employed outside MP courts), educational mark sheets/degrees, and other supporting documents as listed in the application checklist.

faq.question: Q10. How will admit cards be issued and when will exam dates be announced?
faq.answer: Admit cards will be uploaded on www.mphc.gov.in at least 7 days before the Preliminary Screening Examination for candidates with complete applications. Dates for preliminary screening, practical exam, and interview will be notified on the High Court website.
